hypotheses.title: LOX Collagen Crosslink Density Maps to Bond Occupation Probability, Creating a Sharp Percolation Phase Transition in T Cell Immune Exclusion
hypotheses.connection: Bond percolation theory → LOX-mediated collagen crosslink density as bond occupation probability p → Sharp immune exclusion threshold in solid tumors
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: LOX crosslink density = bond occupation probability p; percolation threshold p_c = immune exclusion threshold
hypotheses.key_prediction: Sharp sigmoidal transition in T cell infiltration at critical collagen crosslink density (p_c ~ 0.21-0.24 for active percolation); immune-hot tumors below p_c, immune-cold above; order parameter exponent beta = 0.41
grounded_claims: LOX-collagen crosslinking mechanism (Nicolas-Boluda 2021, eLife)
LOX inhibition improves T cell infiltration (PMID 38267662, 38305736)
Percolation threshold p_c = 0.2488 for 3D simple cubic (Stauffer & Aharony 1994)
Collagen threshold for T cell function (Kuczek 2019)
T cell squeeze threshold 3 um (Wolf et al. 2013)
Active percolation framework 2D (Saha 2024, Soft Matter)
parametric_claims: Active p_c in 3D ~ 0.21-0.24 (extrapolated from 2D)
Lattice mapping of disordered collagen network
Sharp transition vs gradual decrease (novel prediction)

hypotheses.title: Universal Critical Exponent nu = 0.88 Predicts T Cell Clustering Length Scale Across Tumor Types Independent of Molecular Details
hypotheses.connection: Percolation universality (critical exponents) → Correlation length xi ~ |p - p_c|^(-0.88) → T cell cluster size scaling law conserved across all solid tumor types
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Percolation universality class — critical exponents independent of lattice details, testable across tumor types
hypotheses.key_prediction: Log(xi) vs log|p - p_c| collapses onto single line with slope -0.88 across breast, pancreatic, colorectal, and lung tumors; correlation length 15-430 um measurable by confocal/MPM
grounded_claims: Percolation critical exponents nu=0.88, beta=0.41, gamma=1.79 in 3D (Stauffer & Aharony 1994)
Universality across lattice types (renormalization group theory)
Correlation length formula xi ~ |p - p_c|^(-nu)
parametric_claims: Cross-tumor universality of T cell clustering
Measurable length scales 15-430 um (depends on lattice spacing assumption a ~ 5 um)
Collagen alignment may change effective dimension

hypotheses.title: Subdiffusive MSD Exponent alpha = 0.53 at the Percolation Threshold Is a Universal Diagnostic Fingerprint for ECM-Mediated Immune Exclusion
hypotheses.connection: Anomalous diffusion on fractal percolation clusters → MSD ~ t^0.53 for T cells at critical ECM density → Diagnostic biomarker distinguishing percolation-driven from chemokine-driven immune exclusion
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Fractal dimension of random walk on percolation cluster (d_w = 3.8) produces universal subdiffusion exponent
hypotheses.key_prediction: Three regimes: alpha = 1.0 (hot), alpha = 0.53 (critical), alpha -> 0 (cold); alpha = 0.53 is universal constant distinguishable from viscoelastic subdiffusion
grounded_claims: Anomalous diffusion exponent 2/d_w on percolation clusters (Alexander & Orbach 1982)
d_w = 3.8 for 3D percolation (Ben-Avraham & Havlin 2000)
T cell single-cell tracking in collagen feasible
Viscoelastic subdiffusion gives alpha = 0.5-0.9 (Metzler & Klafter 2000)
parametric_claims: Three-regime prediction for biological system
Active particle MSD correction (unknown for 3D)
Alpha = 0.53 distinguishable from other subdiffusion mechanisms

hypotheses.title: BAPN Dose-Response Predicts a Sharp Nonlinear Phase Transition in Immune Infiltration — LOX Inhibitor as Percolation Control Knob
hypotheses.connection: Percolation control parameter tuning → BAPN dose-response as p(dose) → Sharp phase transition in T cell infiltration at critical dose
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: BAPN (LOX inhibitor) directly controls bond occupation probability p; dose-response curve shape encodes percolation physics
hypotheses.key_prediction: T cell infiltration vs BAPN dose is sharp sigmoid with power-law onset exponent beta = 0.41, NOT Hill-equation pharmacology; different tumor types show same exponent but different critical doses
grounded_claims: BAPN irreversibly inhibits LOX (Tang et al. 2017)
BAPN + anti-PD-1 improves T cell infiltration (Nicolas-Boluda 2021)
MMP-mediated crosslink turnover timescale hours-days
Beta = 0.41 as order parameter exponent (percolation theory)
parametric_claims: Phase transition shape prediction (sharp sigmoid, not Hill curve)
p(dose) function (unknown, needs calibration)
Same exponent across tumor types (universality assumption)

hypotheses.title: Finite-Size Scaling Predicts That Biopsy Dimensions Systematically Bias Immune Exclusion Scoring
hypotheses.connection: Finite-size scaling theory → Biopsy size L as system size → Systematic L-dependent shift in apparent immune exclusion threshold
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Percolation finite-size scaling: p_c(L) - p_c(infinity) ~ L^(-1/nu); biopsy = finite system
hypotheses.key_prediction: Inter-biopsy variance in immune score scales as L^(-0.73) for tumors near exclusion threshold; FNA > core needle > excision variance
grounded_claims: Finite-size scaling theory (Stauffer & Aharony 1994; Fisher 1971)
Core-needle biopsy dimensions 1-2 mm diameter
FNA dimensions ~0.7 mm
parametric_claims: Lattice spacing ~ 5 um (estimated)
Variance scaling L^(-0.73) (derived from percolation theory)
Requires tumors near p_c for relevance

hypotheses.title: MMP/LOX Kinetic Balance Creates Dynamic Percolation, Generating Temporal Windows of Immune Infiltration in Tumors
hypotheses.connection: Dynamic percolation theory → MMP degradation/LOX crosslinking kinetics as dynamic p(t) → Temporal immune infiltration windows in solid tumors
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Time-dependent bond occupation p(t) from MMP/LOX kinetic balance; transient subcritical windows permit infiltration
hypotheses.key_prediction: T cell infiltration events cluster in time correlating with MMP activity peaks (r > 0.5); peri-tumoral T cell trapping explained by window closure during transit
grounded_claims: LOX creates crosslinks (Nicolas-Boluda 2021)
MMPs degrade collagen (Lu et al. 2011)
LOX-TGFB1 STRING 0.623, LOX-IL1B STRING 0.727
Peri-tumoral T cell accumulation (Salmon et al. 2012)
T cell migration speed 5-15 um/min (Krummel et al. 2016)
parametric_claims: p(t) = k_LOX/(k_LOX + k_MMP) model (simplified)
Pulsatile infiltration prediction
Timescale matching (MMP hours ~ T cell transit hours)

hypotheses.title: Percolation Backbone Fraction Predicts the Ratio of Functionally Cytotoxic to Trapped T Cells Within Infiltrated Tumors
hypotheses.connection: Percolation backbone vs dangling end topology → Functional vs trapped T cells → Backbone fraction as immunotherapy response biomarker
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Backbone carries through-paths (functional T cells), dangling ends are dead-end branches (trapped T cells); B ~ (p - p_c)^1.05
hypotheses.key_prediction: Backbone fraction of T cells (not total density) predicts immunotherapy response in warm tumors; backbone T cells show higher granzyme B, dangling-end T cells show higher PD-1/LAG-3
grounded_claims: Backbone fraction theory (Herrmann & Stanley 1984; Porto et al. 1997)
Beta_B ~ 1.05 in 3D
Warm tumors as clinical entity (Galon & Bruni 2019)
Granzyme B, PD-1, LAG-3 as functional/exhaustion markers
parametric_claims: Backbone = functional, dangling end = trapped mapping
Exhaustion from physical confinement (not demonstrated)
Backbone fraction as better predictor than density

hypotheses.title: Chemotaxis Breaks Percolation Universality — Directed T Cell Migration Shifts the System to Directed Percolation with Distinct Exponents
hypotheses.connection: Directed percolation universality class → Chemokine-driven migration → Different critical exponents define framework applicability limits
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Chemotactic bias (Pe ~ 3) shifts system from isotropic (nu = 0.88) toward directed percolation (nu_parallel = 1.73, nu_perp = 1.10)
hypotheses.key_prediction: Anisotropic correlation lengths: xi_parallel > xi_perp in tumors with strong chemokine gradients; ratio xi_parallel/xi_perp diverges near p_c; Pe < 1 tumors show isotropic percolation, Pe > 1 show directed
grounded_claims: Directed percolation exponents (Hinrichsen 2000, Adv Phys)
T cell chemotaxis toward CXCL9/10 (Nagarsheth et al. 2017)
Pe ~ 3 estimate (computational validation)
parametric_claims: Isotropic-to-directed crossover at Pe ~ 1
Anisotropic correlation length prediction
Complex chemokine gradients vs unidirectional bias
